watchFor: AVOID: Feb 10-13 represents a catastrophic 4-day losing streak (-$10,382) — this suggests no hard daily loss limit was enforced or respected.
AVOID: Win rate of 37.9% over 29 trades is a structural problem, not a variance problem — the system or trade selection criteria needs reassessment.
AVOID: Losses of -$4,757 and -$2,870 on single days indicate position sizing is not being scaled to account risk properly.
AVOID: Multiple days show 0 day trades but the data still logs them — ensure your tracking is capturing all activity accurately, including swing account moves.
tomorrowRules: Before placing any trade, confirm market regime is TRENDING or clearly directional — do NOT trade NEUTRAL or CHOPPY opens without a specific, pre-defined setup trigger.
Set a hard daily loss limit of $500 max for tomorrow. If hit, close the platform. No exceptions — the Feb 10-13 data shows what happens without this rule.
Only take trades that match the Feb 6 setup profile — high-conviction, regime-aligned, with defined entry and target before execution.
If you take a loss on the first trade, reduce size by 50% for the remainder of the session — do not size up to recover.
Log the specific reason for every trade entry in real time — if you cannot articulate the edge in one sentence before entry, do not take the trade.

dailyLessons.gradingReason: No trades executed today in a NEUTRAL market regime — this is disciplined capital preservation, not inaction. The $57.29 P&L gain in the day trade account with zero trades suggests unrealized position movement or fees/adjustments. Sitting out on a low-conviction day is correct behavior. However, the broader cumulative picture (37.9% win rate, -$10k+ in losses from Feb 10-13) demands attention and pulls the grade below A.
keyLessons: Staying flat on a NEUTRAL market day is a valid and profitable strategy — you avoided unnecessary risk when edge was unclear.
Your cumulative win rate of 37.9% is below the 50% threshold needed without a strong R:R ratio advantage — audit your average winner vs. average loser immediately.
Feb 10-13 produced -$10,382 across 7 trades — identify whether those losses share a common setup, time of day, or market condition so you can create a hard filter.
Feb 6 produced +$20,632 in 8 trades — reverse-engineer that day. What conditions were present? That setup profile should be your primary target going forward.
You have 29 total trades with 18 losses — your system is currently losing more often than it wins, which means your winners MUST be significantly larger than your losers to stay profitable long-term.

goodPatterns: Correctly identified today's NEUTRAL regime and chose not to force trades — capital preservation executed properly.
Coming off a green day on Feb 17 (+$224) without overtrading into Feb 18 shows you are not chasing momentum blindly.
Current streak is green — you are not in a revenge-trading spiral despite the brutal Feb 10-13 drawdown.

dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Today's risk management was good by omission — no trades meant no exposure in a low-edge environment. However, the cumulative data reveals a serious risk management failure in prior weeks. Losses of -$4,757 and -$2,870 on individual days indicate either no stop losses, stops too wide, or position sizing that does not account for account equity percentage. With $90k equity, a single-day loss of -$4,757 represents a 5.3% drawdown — far beyond acceptable 1-2% daily risk thresholds for a disciplined trader.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: No direct evidence of revenge trading or FOMO today — flat day in flat market shows restraint. However, the Feb 10-13 sequence is a red flag: four consecutive losing days with escalating losses suggests emotional escalation (doubling down or revenge trading) rather than systematic stop-outs. Watch carefully tomorrow — coming off a winning day can create overconfidence bias. The prior losing streak may also create an urge to 'make it back quickly.' Stay process-focused, not P&L-focused.
